responsibilities: Design and architect hardware systems including schematics, PCB layouts, and component selection for new product development.
Lead hardware bring-up activities, debugging prototype boards using oscilloscopes, logic analyzers, and JTAG debuggers.
Develop hardware requirements specifications and architecture documents translating product requirements into testable hardware designs.
Collaborate cross-functionally with firmware, mechanical, and industrial design teams to deliver integrated hardware products.
Select and qualify electronic components, balancing performance, cost, availability, and long-term supply chain reliability.
Conduct bench testing and validation of hardware prototypes against functional, electrical, and environmental specifications.
Drive design for manufacturing (DFM) and design for testability (DFT) reviews to ensure smooth production scale-up.
Investigate and resolve field failures and hardware defects through root-cause analysis and corrective design changes.
Create and maintain hardware design documentation including schematics, BOMs, and test procedures throughout the product lifecycle.
Coordinate with contract manufacturers and suppliers during prototype builds and mass production ramp-up.
